claim: OpenAI's revenue is directly constrained by GPU/compute capacity; they can 3x compute year-over-year and revenue follows the same trajectory (2B to 6B to 20B+)
actionable: Understand that OpenAI's growth ceiling is determined by chip supply and compute scaling, not product-market fit or demand
source_title: OpenAI just made a genius move

claim: OpenAI is almost certainly losing money on most tokens generated; profitability estimates suggest 20 billion loss this year despite 20 billion revenue
actionable: Recognize that OpenAI operates at massive losses; their $8/month plan is a loss leader to lock in users, not a profitable product
source_title: OpenAI just made a genius move

claim: Loss leader strategy: OpenAI's $8/month ChatGPT Go plan captures markets with lower purchasing power while data collection and lock-in compound long-term value
actionable: If operating an AI platform, use loss leader pricing in emerging markets to build user lock-in and data moats
source_title: OpenAI just made a genius move

claim: Advertising on ChatGPT free tier could generate 57 billion per year if monetized at Meta's ARPU rates, making it OpenAI's highest-leverage revenue stream
actionable: Consider ads as a second revenue flywheel on top of subscriptions; user data and personalization make ads highly valuable in AI agents
source_title: OpenAI just made a genius move

claim: Inference, not training, is where the money is long-term; as demand for inference scales, so do margins (if efficiency improves)
actionable: Invest in inference optimization and efficient serving; this is the unit economics lever, not model training
source_title: OpenAI just made a genius move

claim: Bundling strategy (offering AI products as add-ons to existing subscriptions) is monopolistic but proven; Microsoft used it against Slack with Teams
actionable: Understand that bundling (like OpenAI may do with future hardware/services) is a distribution moat, not a product quality advantage
source_title: OpenAI just made a genius move
